hypothesis: Baseline: regression on raw fwd_30m excess, all 18 features. Reproduces the trainer result; reference point.
label: raw

hypothesis: Rank label should align loss with how we trade (deciles) and be fat-tail robust; expect >= raw IC.
label: rank

hypothesis: Drop the 5 micro features (identity-leak risk); 13-feature universe set. Should hold IC while removing leakage.
label: rank

hypothesis: Regression on 13 non-micro features; isolates whether micro columns were carrying (leaked) signal.
label: raw

hypothesis: For-fun long shot: does the 60m horizon rank model show anything different? (sparse 60m labels expected; exploratory).
label: rank

hypothesis: (A) Re-run raw/13 WITH gain importances to diagnose WHICH features carry the signal — start of feature-improvement work.
label: raw

hypothesis: Fair 60m comparison: RAW label, 13 features (vs the earlier 60m-rank long-shot). Does a longer horizon help raw regression?
label: raw

hypothesis: (A) Importances show calendar features (day_of_week, minute_of_day) rank high, but they're constant within a cross-section so can't discriminate names. Drop calendar+micro (11 features): if within-ts IC survives, the signal is real cross-sectional; if it collapses, the IC was a time-of-day artifact.
label: raw

hypothesis: Modeller: 60m raw, no-calendar (11 feats). Does ANY price signal survive at the longer horizon without the calendar crutch?
label: raw

hypothesis: Modeller: rank label, no-calendar 11 feats. Honest within-ts cross-sectional test of price features under a trading-aligned-ish loss.
label: rank

hypothesis: KEY TEST: v1.1.0 daily-momentum features, raw, NO calendar. Does cross-sectional momentum give non-artifact within-ts IC where intraday price gave ~0?
label: raw

hypothesis: v1.1.0 momentum + all (incl calendar). Compare to nocalendar to see momentum's standalone contribution vs the calendar crutch.
label: raw

hypothesis: v1.1.0 momentum at 60m horizon, no calendar (momentum decays slower than 30m noise).
label: raw

hypothesis: Overnight (close->next-open) under the net-of-cost gate: ~1 rebalance/day = far lower turnover; should clear breakeven where 30-min could not. raw, no-calendar, v1.1.0 (momentum+price at 15:30).
label: raw

hypothesis: Overnight, all features incl calendar/momentum, v1.1.0. Compare to nocalendar.
label: raw

hypothesis: DEEP panel: overnight + VOL-SCALED label (stops ranking volatility instead of alpha) + no-calendar. Judge NET P&L.
label: vol_scaled

hypothesis: DEEP panel: 30m vol-scaled, no-calendar — does vol-normalization rescue any intraday signal at real depth? NET P&L.
label: vol_scaled

hypothesis: DEEP: overnight + true LGBMRanker (lambdarank, grouped by ts) + no-calendar. Loss aligned to decile trading. NET P&L.
label: lambdarank

hypothesis: DEEP: 30m lambdarank, no-calendar — the trading-aligned loss on real depth. NET P&L.
label: lambdarank

hypothesis: Clean v1.1.1 30m baseline: raw, no-calendar (19 feats). Anchor the per-feature interrogation; expect IC ~0.027 net-negative matching battery.
label: raw

hypothesis: Clean v1.1.1 30m: rank label, no-calendar. Trading-aligned loss; does rank beat raw IC on clean data?
label: rank

hypothesis: Clean v1.1.1 30m raw, ALL 21 incl calendar. Quantify how much of any IC is the within-ts-constant calendar crutch (should add ~0 within-ts).

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: gap_from_open ALONE at overnight. Does the open gap predict overnight continuation/reversal? Pure gap play.
label: raw

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: vol features ONLY (vol_30m,vol_60m,vol_z_30) at 30m rank. Does volatility itself rank future returns (low-vol anomaly intraday)?
label: rank

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: range_pct + vol_z_30 at overnight. Do high-range/high-vol-spike names mean-revert overnight?
label: raw

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: ALL 21 feats, lambdarank, 60m. Throw everything at LTR at a mid horizon — does the ranking objective find structure regression misses?
label: lambdarank

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: relative momentum vol_scaled at 60m. Vol-normalized momentum at a horizon between 30m noise and overnight.
label: vol_scaled

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: ret_60m ALONE at overnight. Does the last hour's return predict overnight? (intraday-to-overnight momentum carryover).
label: raw

hypothesis: ret_5m + position group at fwd_60m raw. Longer horizon = lower turnover. Does the strongest carrier set clear breakeven (toward ~2bps) where 30m could not, purely via reduced turnover?
label: raw

hypothesis: ret_5m + position group at fwd_60m RANK. Turnover-vs-signal: rank loss + 60m horizon on the carrier set — the most economically-favorable price config we can build.
label: rank

hypothesis: Position group + vol group at overnight raw. W11 showed vol@overnight IC 0.0227 (t2.9) and pos@overnight 0.011 — do they combine? (Note: overnight wins must pass survivorship demean.)
label: raw

hypothesis: Position + vol group at overnight rank. Same combination under trading-aligned loss. Flag for survivorship neutralization if breakeven clears cost.
label: rank

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: full nocalendar set, lambdarank, fwd_60m. Trading-aligned loss at lower turnover on the whole price panel — does loss alignment + horizon rescue net P&L?
label: lambdarank

hypothesis: LONG-SHOT: position group, vol_scaled label, 30m. Does vol-normalizing the target sharpen the position-group signal (rank alpha not volatility)?
label: vol_scaled

hypothesis: GAP: pos group at fwd_30m under LAMBDARANK (the only untested label per group; trading-aligned loss historically gave the only positive overnight results). Does loss alignment lift the pos carrier's net P&L vs raw/rank?
label: lambdarank

label: lambdarank
